entries.what_they_read: I read the top line — 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats' — and then immediately scanned for the catch near the $100 button. Found the small print: 'no real money · no seed phrase.'
entries.their_interpretation: Okay so it's fake money, fake trading, NBA players as the underlying thing — basically a fantasy sports simulator dressed up in stock market language. The 'Devnet' label and 'Practice mode' banners suggest this is a demo build, not a live product.
entries.their_decision: I haven't clicked anything yet and I'm not sure I will — I'm going to read the Terms and the Methodology links first.
entries.their_rationale: The '$100' framing still pattern-matches to every phishing page I've ever seen, even with the 'no real money' disclaimer right under it. I need to see who built this and whether the methodology link goes somewhere real before I hand over any click that might trigger an account creation.
confusion_notes: 'Devnet' — is this blockchain terminology? That word is doing a lot of work and it's not explained anywhere on this screen.
'No seed phrase' — why mention seed phrases at all if there's no crypto? Now I'm wondering if there IS crypto involved and they're just downplaying it.
'The gap is your edge' — sounds like trading copy, not a game description. Feels like they're trying to make fake money feel real, which is either clever or manipulative.
'AWAITING TIP-OFF · Prices move after tonight's games' — is this live data? Is there a server? Who's running this?
Step 03 says 'Buying raises the price for the next person' — that is literally how a pump works. Red flag phrasing even if the intent is innocent.
'Built by FanShare' — that's not a trust signal, that's a tautology. Who IS FanShare?
first_impression_elements: Big bold 'Claim $100 →' button in what looks like an accent color — that's the first thing my eye went to and also the first thing I distrusted
'No real money · no seed phrase' in small text directly under the button — I did actually read this, it slowed my skepticism slightly
'Devnet' badge next to the FanShare logo in the header — I noticed it immediately and it raised more questions than it answered
ignored_elements: The LeBron $5.59 vs $4.20 example — I skimmed past it, felt like marketing filler
'💬Feedback' button in the corner — didn't register at all
The 'Thesis' section ('Being right early pays better than being right once') — felt like startup manifesto copy, tuned it out
The three-step 'How it works' numbered list — I clocked the headings but didn't read the body text yet

first_impression_elements: Bold 'real stats.' in orange — the color contrast is doing heavy lifting
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' status bar in orange feels live and urgent but also unfinished
Big yellow 'Claim $100' CTA button — immediately suspicious of what I'm actually claiming
ignored_elements: The faint grid lines in the background — decorative noise
'How it works' secondary link — didn't pull my eye at all
Hamburger menu — no reason to trust there's anything behind it
data.visual_notes: Dark grid aesthetic is fine but feels like a crypto app cosplaying as a sports product — no logos, no team branding, no faces, nothing that says 'real.' DEVNET in the nav is a red flag I can't unsee; that's developer infrastructure language leaking into a consumer page. Zero social proof, zero identity beyond a wordmark, and 'no real money' buried at the bottom reads like a disclaimer, not a feature.
